questions.question: What does the `$PATH` environment variable contain?
options: A) The current working directory
B) The list of directories where shell commands are searched for executables
C) The list of files in the current directory
D) The list of environment variables
questions.explanation: The $PATH variable stores a list of directories where the shell looks for executables when running a command.

questions.question: What is the difference between an ordinary variable and an environment variable in shell programming?
options: A) Ordinary variables are used for storing data, while environment variables are used for storing system settings
B) Ordinary variables are set by the user, while environment variables are set by the system
C) Ordinary variables are global, while environment variables are local
D) Ordinary variables are immutable, while environment variables are mutable
questions.explanation: Ordinary variables are user-defined, global variables, while environment variables are system-defined, global variables.

questions.question: How do you access the value of an environment variable in shell scripting?
options: A) Using the `$` symbol followed by the variable name
B) Using the `${` symbol followed by the variable name
C) Using the `export` command followed by the variable name
D) Using the `unset` command followed by the variable name
questions.explanation: You can access the value of an environment variable using the `${variable_name}` syntax.

questions.question: Can you change the value of an environment variable in shell scripting?
options: A) No, environment variables are immutable
B) Yes, using the `export` command
C) Yes, using the `${variable_name}=(new_value)` syntax
D) Yes, using the `unset` command
questions.explanation: You can change the value of an environment variable using the `${variable_name}=(new_value)` syntax or the `export` command.

questions.question: What happens if you try to unset an environment variable that doesn't exist?
options: A) The shell will display an error message
B) The shell will ignore the command
C) The shell will create a new variable with the same name
D) The shell will set the variable to an empty string
questions.explanation: If you try to unset a non-existent environment variable, the shell will display an error message.
